perturbation.original_text: Promptly upon the request of Licensor, the Licensee shall, at the Licensor's sole discretion and election for each country of the Territory and each Licensed Product either withdraw or transfer all Marketing Authorizations in the Territory to the Licensor (or to a third party as the Licensor directs). Licensee shall take the required steps without any delay and the withdrawal or transfer shall be completed in maximum 90 days after the notification of the request; if the Licensor does not decide and inform within this period to whom the Marketing Authorizations and documents shall be transferred, then the Licensee shall have no obligation to keep them active.
perturbation.changed_text: Promptly upon the request of Licensor, the Licensee shall, at the Licensor's sole discretion and election for each country of the Territory and each Licensed Product either withdraw or transfer all Marketing Authorizations in the Territory to the Licensor (or to a third party as the Licensor directs). Licensee shall take the required steps without any delay and the withdrawal or transfer shall be completed in maximum 30 days after the notification of the request; if the Licensor does not decide and inform within this period to whom the Marketing Authorizations and documents shall be transferred, then the Licensee shall have no obligation to keep them active.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the transfer timeframe for marketing authorizations to 30 days may not be sufficient to comply with regulatory requirements in some Latin American countries, conflicting with local laws.
perturbation.law_explanation: RDC 25/2005, while not explicitly setting a timeframe for MA transfers, establishes procedures and requirements for changes in MA ownership. Reducing the timeframe to 30 days could be insufficient to gather required documentation, notify authorities, and fulfill all steps stipulated by ANVISA, hindering full compliance with the RDC's procedural requirements, potentially conflicting with Brazilian health regulatory law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Página 53 do Diário Oficial da União - Seção 1, número 62, de 01/04/2005 - Imprensa Nacional
perturbation.justification: The modification reduces the timeframe for transferring marketing authorizations from 90 to 30 days. The Law Explanation suggests this timeframe could be insufficient to comply with RDC 25/2005 requirements, specifically gathering documentation and notifying authorities as required by ANVISA. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies Legal Contradiction,' where a time-sensitive obligation does not align with legal requirements, potentially making the contract unenforceable.
